title: Document open-source security model (bring-your-own-keys)
body:
## Context

PR #331 removed all platform authentication (OAuth, OIDC, entitlements) for the open-source release. The security model has changed from platform-managed to user-managed. This should be documented clearly.

## What to document

- **Bring-your-own-keys model**: Users provide their own API keys (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, etc.) and are responsible for securing them
- **Cost responsibility**: LLM API costs are the user's responsibility — bop makes API calls using the user's keys
- **GitHub access**: Repository access is controlled by the `GITHUB_TOKEN` scope, not by bop
- **MCP server trust model**: The MCP server runs via stdio transport (local subprocess) — the host application is the trust boundary
- **Extension points**: `RepoAccessChecker` interface preserved at the usecase layer for future access control implementations

## Suggested location

Update `docs/SECURITY.md` and/or the main `README.md` with a security model section.

## Origin

Code review finding on PR #331 (fingerprint: `a7d3e19d7fd528af1a635471a25f0936`).
